source.title: 2019 ACC/AHA Guideline on the Primary Prevention of Cardiovascular Disease

extraction.summary: The 2019 ACC/AHA guideline provides comprehensive recommendations for primary prevention of atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease (ASCVD) in adults through lifestyle modifications, risk assessment, and targeted pharmacotherapy. Key elements include promoting healthy lifestyle habits throughout life, team-based care approaches, and risk-based decision making for interventions like statin therapy, blood pressure management, and aspirin use.

population.age_criteria: Adults ≥18 years of age, with specific focus on 40-75 years for cardiovascular risk assessment
population.risk_criteria: Primary prevention population without established ASCVD; includes adults with diabetes, hypertension, hyperlipidemia, obesity, tobacco use
population.disease_definition: Prevention of ASCVD outcomes including acute coronary syndromes, MI, stable/unstable angina, arterial revascularization, stroke, TIA, peripheral arterial disease, heart failure, and atrial fibrillation
population.exclusions: Adults with established cardiovascular disease (secondary prevention)
population.severity_staging: Risk stratification using 10-year ASCVD risk estimation tools, with consideration of risk-enhancing factors and coronary artery calcium scoring

key_thresholds.context: Primary age range for 10-year ASCVD risk estimation and pharmacotherapy decisions
General target for adults requiring pharmacological therapy
Level indicating statin therapy for primary prevention
Use pooled cohort equations for 10-year ASCVD risk calculation
Consider additional risk-enhancing factors and coronary calcium scoring
moderate-intensity for additional ASCVD risk reduction
Severe hypercholesterolemia requiring maximally tolerated statin
Intermediate risk threshold for statin consideration
Threshold for more intensive BP treatment
Minimum supportive follow-up period after discharge for hospitalized adults who use tobacco
Recommended distance from entryways, windows, and building vents for smoking restrictions
Time period after tobacco cessation for risk of heart failure and death to approach that of never smokers
Pooled cohort equations for 10-year ASCVD risk prediction
Minimum recommended physical activity for cardiovascular benefit
Ownership of voting stock/shares or fair market value
Funds received from business entity in previous year

recommendations.recommendation: Promote healthy lifestyle throughout life as most important prevention strategy
recommendations.notes: Includes diet, physical activity, tobacco avoidance

recommendations.recommendation: Team-based care approach for ASCVD risk factor control
recommendations.notes: Multidisciplinary health professionals to improve quality and maintenance of prevention

recommendations.recommendation: Shared decision-making should guide ASCVD risk reduction strategies
recommendations.notes: Collaborative discussions about personalized risk and treatment options

recommendations.recommendation: Assess social determinants of health to inform treatment decisions
recommendations.notes: Tailor advice to socioeconomic, educational, cultural contexts

recommendations.recommendation: Consume healthy diet emphasizing vegetables, fruits, nuts, whole grains, lean protein, fish
recommendations.notes: Minimize trans fats, red meat, processed meats, refined carbohydrates, sweetened beverages

recommendations.recommendation: Engage in at least 150 minutes/week moderate-intensity or 75 minutes/week vigorous-intensity physical activity
recommendations.notes: Accumulated physical activity recommendation for all adults

recommendations.recommendation: Use team-based care for ASCVD prevention involving multidisciplinary health professionals
recommendations.notes: Improves quality and maintenance of ASCVD prevention

recommendations.recommendation: Engage patients in shared decision-making about personalized ASCVD risk and prevention strategies
recommendations.notes: Collaborative decisions address potential treatment barriers

recommendations.recommendation: Routinely assess traditional cardiovascular risk factors and calculate 10-year ASCVD risk using PCE for adults 40-75 years
recommendations.notes: Foundation for matching preventive intervention intensity to absolute risk

recommendations.recommendation: Diet emphasizing vegetables, fruits, legumes, nuts, whole grains, and fish
recommendations.notes: Decreases ASCVD risk factors

recommendations.recommendation: Avoid trans fats intake as part of healthy diet
recommendations.notes: Should be avoided to reduce ASCVD risk

recommendations.recommendation: Adults should be routinely counseled in healthcare visits to optimize a physically active lifestyle
recommendations.notes: Number needed to counsel as low as 12

recommendations.recommendation: Adults should engage in at least 150 minutes per week of moderate-intensity or 75 minutes per week of vigorous-intensity aerobic physical activity
recommendations.notes: To reduce ASCVD risk

recommendations.recommendation: Counseling and comprehensive lifestyle interventions, including calorie restriction, are recommended for achieving weight loss in adults with overweight and obesity
recommendations.notes: High-intensity interventions (≥14 sessions in 6 months) work best

recommendations.recommendation: For adults with T2DM, it is reasonable to initiate metformin as first-line therapy along with lifestyle therapies
recommendations.notes: At time of diagnosis to improve glycemic control and reduce ASCVD risk

recommendations.recommendation: Heart-healthy dietary pattern for all adults with T2DM to improve glycemic control and ASCVD risk factors
recommendations.notes: Mediterranean, DASH, and vegetarian diets shown to be effective

recommendations.recommendation: At least 150 minutes/week moderate-intensity or 75 minutes vigorous-intensity physical activity for T2DM
recommendations.notes: Combination of aerobic and resistance training preferred

recommendations.recommendation: Moderate-intensity statin therapy for intermediate risk patients (≥7.5% to <20% 10-year ASCVD risk)
recommendations.notes: Requires risk discussion with patient

recommendations.recommendation: Moderate-intensity statin therapy for adults 40-75 years with diabetes regardless of 10-year risk
recommendations.notes: Applies to both type 1 and type 2 diabetes

recommendations.recommendation: Nonpharmacological interventions for elevated BP/hypertension including weight loss, DASH diet, sodium reduction, potassium supplementation, physical activity, and limited alcohol
recommendations.notes: Recommended for all adults with elevated BP or hypertension

recommendations.recommendation: BP-lowering medications for adults with 10-year ASCVD risk ≥10% and average SBP ≥130 or DBP ≥80 mmHg
recommendations.notes: For primary prevention of CVD

recommendations.recommendation: BP target of <130/80 mmHg for adults with confirmed hypertension and 10-year ASCVD risk ≥10%
recommendations.notes: Recommended BP goal

recommendations.recommendation: All adults should be assessed for tobacco use at every healthcare visit with tobacco status recorded as vital sign
recommendations.notes: To facilitate tobacco cessation

recommendations.recommendation: Combination of behavioral interventions plus pharmacotherapy to maximize tobacco quit rates
recommendations.notes: For adults who use tobacco

recommendations.recommendation: Adults who use tobacco should be asked about tobacco use and urged to stop using tobacco
recommendations.notes: The best treatments are those acceptable and feasible for individual patients

recommendations.recommendation: Healthcare professionals should receive training in tobacco treatment
recommendations.notes: Trained professionals are more likely to provide effective interventions and increase quit rates

recommendations.recommendation: For hospitalized adults who use tobacco, intensive counseling with continued supportive follow-up for at least one month after discharge
recommendations.notes: Evidence for this intervention in pregnant women is substantial

recommendations.recommendation: ENDS should not be used as tobacco treatment method
recommendations.notes: Evidence unclear about effectiveness and potentially harmful

recommendations.recommendation: Use team-based care interventions for hypertension management
recommendations.notes: Meta-analysis shows potency of team-based care interventions

recommendations.recommendation: Assess cardiovascular risk using pooled cohort equations
recommendations.notes: 2013 ACC/AHA guideline on cardiovascular risk assessment

recommendations.recommendation: Consider coronary artery calcium scoring for risk assessment
recommendations.notes: CAC score improves risk assessment above traditional risk factors

recommendations.recommendation: Mediterranean diet supplemented with extra-virgin olive oil or nuts for primary prevention
recommendations.notes: Based on PREDIMED study results

recommendations.recommendation: Regular physical activity of at least 150 minutes per week of moderate intensity aerobic activity
recommendations.notes: Dose-response relationship demonstrated for cardiovascular risk reduction

recommendations.recommendation: Weight management and obesity treatment through lifestyle interventions
recommendations.notes: Includes behavioral interventions and consideration of bariatric surgery for severe obesity

recommendations.recommendation: Intensive glucose control in type 2 diabetes patients
recommendations.notes: Metformin as first-line therapy, with consideration of SGLT2 inhibitors and GLP-1 agonists

recommendations.recommendation: Statin therapy for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
recommendations.notes: Based on multiple randomized controlled trials including WOSCOPS, JUPITER, and others

recommendations.recommendation: Intensive blood pressure lowering in certain populations
recommendations.notes: Based on SPRINT and ACCORD trials

recommendations.recommendation: Lifestyle interventions for blood pressure control
recommendations.notes: Including DASH diet, sodium reduction, weight loss, and exercise

recommendations.recommendation: Intensive blood pressure lowering for cardiovascular risk reduction
recommendations.notes: Based on systematic reviews and network meta-analyses

recommendations.recommendation: Tobacco cessation interventions including behavioral counseling and pharmacotherapy
recommendations.notes: Combined interventions more effective than single approaches

recommendations.recommendation: Low-dose aspirin for primary prevention in selected patients
recommendations.notes: Benefits must outweigh bleeding risks

recommendations.recommendation: Low-dose aspirin for primary prevention of atherosclerotic events
recommendations.strength: Variable based on individual risk assessment
recommendations.evidence_level: Based on randomized controlled trials and systematic reviews
recommendations.notes: Requires shared decision making and consideration of bleeding risk

recommendations.recommendation: Behavioral counseling for healthy diet and physical activity
recommendations.notes: For cardiovascular disease prevention in adults without known risk factors

recommendations.recommendation: Tobacco use cessation interventions
recommendations.notes: Including behavioral and pharmacotherapy interventions

medications.indication: First-line therapy for adults with type 2 diabetes
Second-line consideration for type 2 diabetes after metformin
Second-line consideration for type 2 diabetes after metformin
First-line treatment for primary prevention with elevated LDL-C ≥190 mg/dL, diabetes (ages 40-75), or sufficient ASCVD risk
Infrequent use in routine primary prevention due to lack of net benefit
T2DM with additional ASCVD risk factors requiring glucose-lowering therapy
T2DM with additional ASCVD risk factors requiring glucose-lowering therapy
Intermediate risk patients and diabetes patients 40-75 years
LDL-C ≥190 mg/dL, diabetes with multiple ASCVD risk factors

medications.drug: Nicotine replacement therapy (patch, gum, lozenge, nasal spray, oral inhaler)
medications.dose: Varies by product and tobacco use level
medications.indication: Tobacco cessation

medications.dose: 150mg SR once daily x3 days, then twice daily
medications.indication: Tobacco cessation

medications.dose: 0.5mg daily x3 days, then 0.5mg twice daily x4 days, then 1mg twice daily
medications.indication: Tobacco cessation
Tobacco dependence when behavioral interventions alone are insufficient
Primary prevention based on risk assessment and guidelines
Prevention of vascular events in men and women with elevated C-reactive protein
Type 2 diabetes with cardiovascular benefits
Type 2 diabetes with cardiovascular benefits

medications.dose: Moderate to high intensity based on risk assessment
medications.indication: LDL cholesterol lowering for cardiovascular risk reduction
Primary prevention of cardiovascular events
Primary prevention of coronary heart disease
Primary prevention of cardiovascular events
Blood pressure lowering
Smoking cessation

red_flags: LDL cholesterol ≥190 mg/dL requiring statin therapy
Active tobacco use requiring cessation assistance
High 10-year ASCVD risk requiring risk discussion and intervention
Chronic inflammatory conditions (rheumatoid arthritis, lupus, HIV)
Chronic kidney disease with eGFR 15-59 mL/min/1.73m²
History of preeclampsia or preterm delivery
Southern dietary pattern (fried foods, added fats, organ and processed meats, sugar-sweetened beverages)
LDL-C ≥190 mg/dL requiring immediate maximally tolerated statin therapy
Diabetes with multiple ASCVD risk factors
Coronary artery calcium score ≥100 or ≥75th percentile in intermediate risk patients
Recent MI, serious arrhythmia, or unstable angina with tobacco cessation medications
History or risk of seizures with bupropion
Severe renal impairment requiring dose adjustment for varenicline
Brief exposure to secondhand smoke can trigger myocardial infarction
No safe lower limit of exposure to secondhand smoke exists
Smoking strongly associated with ASCVD in young people
Premature coronary heart disease family history
Diabetes mellitus with cardiovascular risk factors
Extreme obesity with cardiovascular comorbidities requiring immediate intervention
Diabetic complications including retinopathy and nephropathy
Major bleeding events with aspirin use
extraction.follow_up: Regular healthcare visits for tobacco use assessment; ongoing monitoring of risk factors and lifestyle interventions
extraction.escalation_path: 10-year ASCVD risk estimation followed by clinician-patient risk discussion before starting pharmacological therapy; consider risk-enhancing factors and coronary artery calcium scoring for intermediate-risk patients
